sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me understand the game better. The bottom panel has:
1. A sky blue strip with 5 symbols (rows 40-46)
2. A pink strip with 5 symbols (rows 51-57)
3. White cursor markers above and below the strips
The cursor selects a position in the pink strip. LEFT/RIGHT moves the cursor, UP/DOWN changes the symbol at that position.
My goal hypothesis: Make the pink strip match the sky blue strip (or match some target from the top grid).
Let me look at the sky blue strip symbols more carefully. From the image, I can see 5 distinct symbols in the sky blue strip. The pink strip also has 5 symbols. The goal is likely to change the pink strip symbols to match the sky blue strip symbols.
Let me now explore the symbol cycling mechanism. I'll move to the first position and cycle through symbols to see all available options, then figure out which one matches the sky blue strip.
First, let me go back to position 0 (leftmost) and explore.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Go back to position 0 (leftmost) and explore symbol cycling
# Currently at position 2 (col ~22-26), need to go LEFT twice
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
